text:
BOTTOM LINE — Energy bid, tech crushed, small caps survived. Oil shock repriced the entire sector deck: XLE surged +3.01% on Trump’s tariff reinstatement, while semis cratered -4.77% as SK Hynix’s 14% debut pop failed to hold the group. Bonds and gold sold off as the dollar strengthened +0.39%, signaling a oil-driven growth scare rotation out of rate-sensitive and cyclical names.

INDICES — VIX 17.2, above its 20d MA of 17.0. Ranking by return: DIA -0.25%, SPY -0.77%, IWM -0.85%, QQQ -1.90%, ^SOX -4.77%. Small > large by 0.38pp. Notable cross-asset: WTI crude +8.36%. Neither SPY nor QQQ is below the 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.8x, QQQ 0.9x — both below average, but QQQ’s vol up + price down = distribution in tech. XLE saw the only elevated volume (1.3x) with price up +3.01% = accumulation in energy. RSP and DIA saw declining volume, indicating low conviction in the broader selloff.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +3.01%, XLU Utilities +0.68%, XLF Financials +0.65%, XLRE Real Estate +0.56%, XLP Staples +0.56%, XLV Healthcare +0.35%, XLC Comms -0.04%, XLB Materials -0.61%, XLI Industrials -0.85%, XLY Discretionary -1.02%, XLK Technology -2.42%. Top Industries: USO +8.36% (oil), MSOS +4.68% (cannabis). Bottom Industries: DRAM -9.11% (memory chips), SOXX -4.77% (semis). Money is rotating out of tech/semis into energy and utilities. Rotation state: 'energy-led rotation off AI hardware'.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 2.16 — extreme protection demand. QQQ skew +17.2% and PCR_vol 1.64 — heavy put premium = fear in tech. VIX above its 20d MA confirms elevated equity vol. GEX flip levels are unknown (missing data), but price near maxpain on SPY (675) and QQQ (641) suggests dealer hedging may cap downside near those levels.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +8.36% vs gold -2.62% — a rare risk-off signal where crude’s supply shock crushed precious metals via stronger dollar and rate-hike expectations. Dollar +0.39% compounding the pressure.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 63.1 (Greed). Top movers: USO +8.36% (oil), DRAM -9.11% (memory chips), SOXX -4.77% (semiconductors). 20d-momentum extremes: best sector XLE -2.59% (still negative 20d but sharp reversal); worst sector DRAM -9.11%. Watch: USO — vol spike (2.3x) and trend divergence vs falling gold; QQQ — elevated put skew and near gamma flip territory at 641; XLE — accumulation volume on the biggest sector gainer.
